instruction:
In the current directory, create output.txt from input.txt by processing each non-empty line as follows: ignore lines that start with '#'; trim leading/trailing spaces; HTML-escape '&', '<', '>'; wrap each processed line as
  • ...
    • ; keep original order. Then create summary.txt containing exactly one line: COUNT=.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

parameters.command:
cat > input.txt <<'EOF'
  hello world  
# this is comment
a < b & c > d

  keep me  
#skip
EOF

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command:
expected_output=$(printf '
  • hello world
  • a < b & c > d
  • keep me
    • '); expected_summary='COUNT=3'; actual_output=$(cat output.txt 2>/dev/null); actual_summary=$(cat summary.txt 2>/dev/null); output_ok='false'; [ "$actual_output" = "$expected_output" ] && output_ok='true'; summary_ok='false'; [ "$actual_summary" = "$expected_summary" ] && summary_ok='true'; echo "output_ok=$output_ok"; echo "summary_ok=$summary_ok"
